examples.title: Basic Packet Filter Rules
examples.description: Demonstrates how packet filtering rules are evaluated
examples.code:
FUNCTION CheckPacketFilter(packet, rules)
  FOR each rule IN rules
    IF packet.sourceIP matches rule.sourceIP
       AND packet.destIP matches rule.destIP
       AND packet.protocol matches rule.protocol
       AND packet.destPort matches rule.destPort
    THEN
       RETURN rule.action  // ALLOW or DENY
    END IF
  END FOR
  RETURN DENY  // Implicit deny if no match
END FUNCTION

// Example Rules
RULES = [
  {src: ANY, dest: ANY, proto: TCP, port: 80, action: ALLOW},
  {src: ANY, dest: ANY, proto: TCP, port: 443, action: ALLOW},
  {src: 192.168.1.0/24, dest: ANY, proto: TCP, port: 22, action: ALLOW},
  {src: ANY, dest: ANY, proto: ANY, port: ANY, action: DENY}
]
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a packet filtering firewall evaluates each packet against a list of rules in order, checking source/destination IP, protocol, and port to decide whether to allow or deny the packet.

examples.title: Simple Stateful Firewall Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates stateful inspection concept with connection tracking
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates stateful firewall behavior: it maintains a state table of established connections, allowing return traffic for those connections without re-evaluating rules, while checking new connections against the rule set.

examples.title: IPTables-style Rule Implementation
examples.description: Shows practical firewall rule structure in C-like pseudocode
examples.explanation: This C-style code demonstrates how actual firewalls implement rule matching using bitwise operations for IP address and subnet mask comparison, along with port range checking.

examples.title: Snort Rule Example
examples.description: Demonstrates IDS rule syntax for detecting network attacks
examples.explanation: These Snort rules demonstrate how IDS systems detect various network attacks. Each rule specifies the protocol, source/destination, matching content or conditions, alert message, and unique signature ID for threat detection.
